question_text: A piece of sandpaper with high surface roughness causes more friction than smooth paper when rubbed against the same material. Which statement best explains why rougher surfaces produce greater friction forces?

options.label: A
options.text: Rough surfaces are heavier and therefore create more friction

options.label: B
options.text: Rough surfaces have more microscopic peaks and valleys that interlock and collide with the other surface, increasing friction

options.label: C
options.text: Rough surfaces are colder and cold objects always produce more friction

options.label: D
options.text: Rough surfaces attract more air molecules, which increases friction

explanation: At a microscopic level, rough surfaces have tiny bumps and dips. When two surfaces slide past each other, these bumps collide and interlock, creating more resistance to movement—this is friction.
